mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force firms to cut output and trigger negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following an industrial production decline exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where inflation accelerates while policy rates do not decline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months following a housing starts drop greater than 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a supply-driven recovery that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of observed cases or loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.0%.
